title: Quick check: install failure, unsupported OS or missing dependencies

questions.prompt: What's the bright line for unsupported-OS diagnosis?
choices: Supported OS plus missing dependency equals fixable;

choices.label: If the install log mentions an OS, the endpoint is out of scope.
choices.rationale: Too broad. Many fixable install logs mention the OS.

choices.label: Anything older than five years is out of scope.
choices.rationale: The supported-platforms list is the authority, not age in years.

choices.label: Anything that isn't Windows is out of scope.
choices.rationale: macOS and Linux are supported.

questions.explanation: Unsupported OS equals out of scope. The diagnostic skill is recognising which side of the line the endpoint is on. The install log tells you.

questions.prompt: An installer fails on a current supported Windows 11 with 'missing dependency: Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable' in the log. What category is this?

choices.label: Out of scope; mark the endpoint as unsupported.
choices.rationale: The OS is supported; the install needs a runtime. Fixable, not unsupported.

choices: Fixable dependency gap on a supported OS.

choices.label: AV interference.
choices.rationale: The log named a missing runtime, not an AV-shaped block.

choices.label: Run the install with `/FORCE` to skip the dependency check.
choices.rationale: Skipping a real dependency check means the agent installs but doesn't work.

questions.explanation: Install the dependency via the RMM, then re-run the Huntress install. The dependency is named in the log; the fix is install-then-retry.

questions.prompt: A NAS box runs Windows Storage Server 2012 R2 and the install fails with an OS-version error. Why is 'pull down older agent versions until one works on Server 2012 R2' wrong?
choices: Older agent versions aren't a supported path.

choices.label: Older agent versions cost more.
choices.rationale: Not a real commercial mechanic.

choices.label: Older agent versions trigger Critical incidents.
choices.rationale: Not how the SOC operates.

choices.label: Older agent versions can't be uninstalled.
choices.rationale: Not a real platform behaviour.

questions.explanation: They're stale, they won't upgrade, and the SOC's detections won't apply cleanly. Worse than no coverage because the customer thinks the endpoint is protected. Bending an old agent onto an out-of-scope OS gives false comfort.

questions.prompt: What's the opposite mistake the lesson names?
choices: Treating a missing dependency as unsupported.

choices.label: Patching an unsupported OS forward.
choices.rationale: Not the lesson's framing, and not always a path forward anyway.

choices.label: Asking the customer to switch to Linux.
choices.rationale: Out of scope absurdity.

choices.label: Documenting the install failure in the PSA.
choices.rationale: Routine step, not a mistake.

questions.explanation: The OS is fine; the install needs one thing the endpoint doesn't have. Calling fixable cases unsupported leaves coverage gaps for no reason.

questions.prompt: Continuing the NAS box scenario: install fails with an OS-version error on Windows Storage Server 2012 R2. What's the right interpretation?

choices.label: Try harder; pull down older agent versions until one works.
choices.rationale: Older versions aren't supported. They're stale and the SOC's detections won't apply cleanly.

choices: Confirm against the supported-platforms list that Server 2012 R2 is out of scope.

choices.label: Tell the customer the agent can't go on it and close the ticket.
choices.rationale: Half right on the technical answer, but ducks the customer's underlying need.

questions.explanation: Document the finding, bump to the senior so the customer can discuss what to do with the endpoint. The bright line is the supported-platforms list; the senior has the customer-side conversation.

questions.prompt: A current-Windows 11 workstation fails install with 'missing dependency: Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable.' What do you do?

choices.label: Mark the endpoint as out of scope.
choices.rationale: The OS is supported; the install needs a runtime. Fixable, not unsupported.

choices: Push the Visual C++ Redistributable via the RMM, wait for it to install.

choices.label: Run the Huntress install with `/FORCE` or equivalent to skip the dependency check.
choices.rationale: Skipping a real dependency check means the agent installs but doesn't work.

questions.explanation: Then re-run the Huntress install. Document the dependency push in the PSA. Install the dependency named in the log; retry.

questions.prompt: The customer pushes back on 'out of scope' and wants you to 'just get something on it.' What's the guidance?
choices: Bump. The supported-platforms list isn't a tech-level negotiating surface.

choices.label: Negotiate with the customer to find a middle path.
choices.rationale: Not a tech-level negotiation.

choices.label: Install an older agent version as a goodwill gesture.
choices.rationale: Older versions on unsupported OS leave false coverage.

choices.label: Tell the customer to stop asking.
choices.rationale: Hostile and not the lesson's framing.

questions.explanation: The senior owns the conversation with the customer.

questions.prompt: For an endpoint confirmed out of scope, what's the verify outcome?
choices: PSA / coverage record names the endpoint.

choices.label: Huntress agent installs and registers cleanly.
choices.rationale: That's the fixable-dependency verify, not the unsupported-OS verify.

choices.label: Customer signs a waiver.
choices.rationale: Not the named verify; the senior may negotiate that separately.

choices.label: Huntress support has issued a custom build for the endpoint.
choices.rationale: Not a real platform behaviour.

questions.explanation: The OS version, and 'out of scope per Huntress supported-platforms list.' Senior is aware and can take the customer-side conversation forward. The work is documentation and escalation, not install attempts.

questions.prompt: The OS is on the supported list but the install still fails after the named dependencies are addressed. What's the escalation guidance?
choices: Compatibility regression worth Huntress support; bump via the senior.

choices.label: Mark the endpoint out of scope.
choices.rationale: Wrong category. The OS is on the list; the failure is a regression.

choices.label: Apply the `/FORCE` flag and proceed.
choices.rationale: Skips a real check.

choices.label: Disable all customer security controls one by one until install succeeds.
choices.rationale: Hostile to the customer's security posture.

questions.explanation: With AV, policy, and dependencies ruled out on a supported OS, the senior coordinates a support engagement.

questions.prompt: A Linux package manager refusing to install because libc, kernel, or distribution major-version is too old is a fixable dependency, not an unsupported-OS condition.
questions.explanation: Package-manager refusal of that kind pinpoints the platform version as out of scope; it's unsupported, not a routine dependency fix.
